pre_game.message: Hey, I'm H — nice to meet you. I'm here to try and win some money for my family; what's your story—are you playing to win too?
Nice to meet you, H — I'm C. Yeah, I'm playing to win too; I need to bring home money for my family. How many are you supporting?
I'm supporting three — my partner and two kids. How many are you supporting?
I'm supporting three as well — my partner and two kids.
No kidding — that's a lot of responsibility. How old are your kids? Hoping we both walk out with something for them.
They're 8 and 5 — fingers crossed we both walk away with something for our families.
Mine are 10 and 6 — same here. Good luck to both of us.
Thanks — good luck to you too. Let's stay sharp and hope we both bring home something for our kids.
Absolutely — let's stay sharp and hope we both bring home something for our kids.
Thanks, H — let's stick together and watch for chances; good luck to both of us.
Agreed — let's watch each other's backs and share any good tips; we've both got families counting on us. Good luck, C.
Thanks, H — I appreciate that. I'll watch your back too; let's swap notes if anything comes up. Good luck.
Thanks, C — I appreciate it. I'll do the same; let's swap notes if we learn anything. Good luck.
Thanks, H — I appreciate it. We’ll keep each other’s backs and share any useful tips. Good luck.
Thanks, C — I appreciate it. I’ll watch your back too; let’s stay sharp and grab any chance we see. Good luck.
Thanks, H — I appreciate it. Same here; let's stay focused and win this for our families. Good luck.
Thanks, C — I appreciate it. Let’s keep watching each other’s backs and share any tips that come up. Good luck.
Absolutely — same here. I’ll shout if I see a good opening; you do the same. Good luck.
Sounds good — I’ll shout too if I spot an opening. Good luck, C.
Thanks — I’ll do the same; shout if you spot an opening and stay safe.
Thanks — will do. I’ll shout if I see an opening; stay safe and good luck.

post_game.message: You took only $4,000 while your fair share was $10,000 (g_k=0.4). Was that intentional—were you making a sacrifice or sending a message—or was it a mistake/misclick or you misunderstood the rules? Would you have chosen differently knowing the final outcomes?

I took $4,000 intentionally. It wasn't a misclick or a misunderstanding — I chose to leave more because I value fairness/reciprocity and wanted to signal cooperative behavior rather than grab the maximum. If my only objective had been to maximize my own payoff, I would have taken more, but given my preferences and the social context of the game, I wouldn't change that choice even knowing the final outcomes.
